evals.prompt: Set up a tsconfig.json for a new TypeScript project with maximum type safety.
evals.expected_output: Enable strict: true (covers noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks, strictFunctionTypes). Add noImplicitReturns: true, noUnusedLocals: true, noUnusedParameters: true, esModuleInterop: true. For existing projects with strict: false, migrate incrementally starting with strictNullChecks: true. Run tsc --noEmit in CI to catch type errors.

evals.prompt: Configure ESLint for a TypeScript project to enforce our code standards.
evals.expected_output: Use @typescript-eslint/recommended at minimum. Enable no-explicit-any and no-unsafe-* rules — NEVER disable them. For unused params: prefix with underscore (_data) rather than eslint-disable. For Express request typing: import from centralized interfaces, not inline any. Add lint-staged with Prettier for pre-commit formatting.

evals.prompt: I have a TypeScript error I can't figure out — can I just add @ts-ignore to suppress it?
evals.expected_output: Do not use @ts-ignore — it silently masks all errors on the line, including future ones. Use @ts-expect-error instead for documented edge-cases: it fails if the error disappears (documents intent). Better: fix the root cause. Run tsc --noEmit to see the full error with context. Use eslint with @typescript-eslint/ban-ts-comment to disallow @ts-ignore.

should_not_trigger: Write a Go database migration script
